deep_dive.title: Mechanobiology-guided drug repurposing identifies budesonide as an inhibitor of stiffness-induced PDAC aggressiveness
deep_dive.abstract: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) develops within a desmoplastic and stiffened microenvironment that critically shapes tumor progression and therapeutic resistance, yet these features are not reproduced by conventional rigid plastic culture systems. Here, we leverage a tuneable bioengineered platform that mimics stromal stiffening to investigate how mechanical cues regulate PDAC cell behaviour and to identify pharmacological strategies that counteract stiffness-driven malignancy. We show that increasing matrix stiffness promotes key hallmarks of PDAC aggressiveness, including enhanced cell spreading, focal adhesions maturation, and cytoskeletal tension. Notably, we identify the glucocorticoid budesonide as a selective suppressor of stiffness-induced malignant phenotypes. Transcriptomic profiling reveals that budesonide counteracts stiffness-associated gene programs, prominently affecting pathways governing cytoskeletal dynamics, nuclear envelope organization, and YAP nucleocytoplasmic transport. Consistently, budesonide reduced force transmission to the nucleus, restoring nuclear wrinkling and constraining nuclear size and shape. These effects are mediated through both glucocorticoid receptor-dependent and -independent mechanisms, revealing a previously unrecognized mode of action. Together, our findings establish mechanical context as a critical determinant of PDAC vulnerability and identify budesonide as a candidate for therapeutic repurposing to target stiffness-driven cancer progression.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: study of tumor microenvironment stiffness driving therapeutic resistance in PDAC, identifies candidate drug
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究肿瘤微环境刚度驱动治疗耐药，在PDAC中鉴定候选药物
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究肿瘤微环境刚度驱动治疗耐药，在PDAC中鉴定候选药物
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究肿瘤微环境刚度驱动治疗耐药，在PDAC中鉴定候选药物
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Using a tunable bioengineered platform mimicking stromal stiffening, this study shows matrix stiffness promotes PDAC aggressiveness and identifies budesonide as an inhibitor of stiffness-driven malignancy, addressing microenvironment-mediated resistance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对胰腺导管腺癌（PDAC）微环境僵硬促进治疗耐药的问题，本文利用可调生物工程平台模拟基质僵硬，发现基质刚度促进细胞铺展、粘着斑成熟和细胞骨架张力等恶性特征。通过药物筛选鉴定出糖皮质激素布地奈德可抑制僵硬驱动的恶性行为，为克服微环境介导的耐药提供了新策略。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: role of tumor microenvironment in neoadjuvant therapy resistance

quick_skim.title: Epigenetic profile drives accurate survival prediction in breast cancer via a multi-omics machine learning model
quick_skim.abstract: Accurate overall survival (OS) prediction is key for personalized treatment in breast cancer, but mutation burden alone is insufficient. To improve prognostic accuracy, we integrated genomic, transcriptomic, proteomic, epigenetic, and clinical features from 802 breast cancer patients to develop BANDOL (Breast cancer Analysis with Neoplastic Data and Omics Learning), a Random Survival Forest model. BANDOL correctly predicted survival ranking in 75% of patient pairs and outperformed mutation-based models (time-dependent AUC: 0.9-1 vs. 0.4-0.9). Immune activation signatures correlated with a longer OS after therapy, while a shorter OS was linked to TREM2 myeloid cells, B cells, and leptin signaling. Model robustness was confirmed in three independent The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA) cohorts (uterine, ovarian and glioma cancers). Epigenetic features were the strongest OS predictors for BANDOL. Current therapies may be combined with strategies to target the methylations of ME3, PPARG, OLIG3 and SLC25A22 genes. This study demonstrates that multi-omics integration via machine learning enhances survival prediction and reveals actionable biomarkers.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-omics machine learning for cancer prognosis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多组学机器学习预后预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多组学机器学习预后预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多组学机器学习预后预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Integrates multi-omics data with Random Survival Forest to predict breast cancer survival, achieving high AUC and revealing immune correlates.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前乳腺癌预后预测主要依赖突变负荷，精度不足。该研究整合基因组、转录组、蛋白质组、表观遗传和临床特征，构建随机生存森林模型BANDOL。BANDOL在75%患者对中正确预测生存排序，时间依赖性AUC达0.9-1，优于传统模型。免疫激活特征与较长生存期相关。该工作展示了多组学机器学习在癌症预后中的强大能力，其方法可直接迁移至药物耐药预测任务。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前乳腺癌预后预测主要依赖突变负荷，精度不足。该研究整合基因组、转录组、蛋白质组、表观遗传和临床特征，构建随机生存森林模型BANDOL。BANDOL在75%患者对中正确预测生存排序，时间依赖性AUC达0.9-1，优于传统模型。免疫激活特征与较长生存期相关。该工作展示了多组学机器学习在癌症预后中的强大能力，其方法可直接迁移至药物耐药预测任务。

quick_skim.title: Transcriptomic data and biomedical literature synergize in finding pharmacologic gene regulators
quick_skim.abstract: Most disorders caused by a deficiency or excess of one gene product lack targeted therapies. Since these disorders can be modeled with a gene overexpression, knockout, or knockdown, drugs that oppose the transcriptomic effects of such perturbations may be promising therapeutic candidates. RNA-Sequencing (RNA-Seq) studies can fuel this drug-prioritization, but their labels, written in plain language, must be annotated manually. Hence, we introduce Signature-based Networks from Automatically Curated Knockout, Knockdown, and Small-molecule Studies (SNACKKSS), which automatically curates gene-disruption and drug-treatment studies from the Gene Expression Omnibus and, in partnership with uniformly computed read count datasets, feeds the labels and RNA-Seq data directly into regulatory relationship predictions. Through cross-validation, we show that SNACKKSS predictions (specifically, from a variation called "SA4") make a unique contribution to finding protein-inhibiting compounds, even alongside existing predictors. We demonstrate the benefit of aggregating multiple predictive tools, and provide this powerful ensemble alongside SNACKKSS. Importantly, we advise researchers to test complex machine learning models on multiple devices, since the hardware architecture can affect their output. Nonetheless, the downstream predictive ability was striking, and our findings support the value of integrating automatically curated RNA-Seq signatures with literature- and co-expression-based predictors for drug-repurposing prioritization.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: transcriptomic data and literature mining for drug target discovery
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 转录组数据和文献挖掘用于药物靶点发现
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 转录组数据和文献挖掘用于药物靶点发现
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 转录组数据和文献挖掘用于药物靶点发现
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SNACKKSS automatically curates gene perturbation and drug studies from GEO, enabling transcriptomics-based identification of gene regulators for drug discovery.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 大多数基因产物异常疾病缺乏靶向疗法。该研究通过自动从GEO筛选基因干扰和药物处理研究，结合统一计算读段计数，构建信号网络以发现药物基因调控因子。该方法无需人工标注，可批量识别逆转疾病转录组状态的候选药物。该框架可应用于肿瘤耐药相关基因调控因子的发现，为生物信息学耐药分析提供新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 大多数基因产物异常疾病缺乏靶向疗法。该研究通过自动从GEO筛选基因干扰和药物处理研究，结合统一计算读段计数，构建信号网络以发现药物基因调控因子。该方法无需人工标注，可批量识别逆转疾病转录组状态的候选药物。该框架可应用于肿瘤耐药相关基因调控因子的发现，为生物信息学耐药分析提供新工具。

quick_skim.title: SMART: A Somatic Mutation Annotation and Reporting Tool for cancer genomics
quick_skim.abstract: Motivation: Translational interpretation of somatic variants from targeted oncology panels is hampered by inconsistent transcript prioritisation and by the need for reproducible pipelines that natively integrate OncoKB-derived evidence for research purposes. Results: We present SMART (Somatic Mutation Annotation and Reporting Tool), a Dockerised pipeline that embeds OncoKB API-derived annotations, including therapeutic (L1-4), resistance (R1-R3), diagnostic (Dx1-3), prognostic (Px1-3) and FDA levels, directly into a VCF-based workflow. SMART combines this with VEP, CIViC, Cancer Hotspots, ClinVar, SpliceAI, REVEL, LOEUF and gnomAD, applies a unified three-tier transcript prioritisation (whitelist > MANE Select > VEP fallback), and produces three-tiered outputs for computational, bioinformatic and research interpretation. Validation against reference APIs showed full concordance across 804 field-level checks. Availability and Implementation: Source code and Docker image are freely available at https://github.com/WeTGI-colab/SMART under the MIT License. SMART is provided for research use only; use of SMART outputs for patient specific clinical reports, clinical decision-making, or other patient-facing purposes requires appropriate governance and all required third-party licensing, including any OncoKB licence required for patient report generation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: bioinformatics tool for resistance mutation annotation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于耐药突变注释的生物信息学工具
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于耐药突变注释的生物信息学工具
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于耐药突变注释的生物信息学工具
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SMART is a Dockerized pipeline that integrates OncoKB, VEP, CIViC, and other resources to annotate somatic variants with therapeutic and resistance levels, enabling reproducible cancer genomics analysis.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: SMART是一个基于Docker的流程，整合OncoKB（含耐药等级R1-R3）、VEP、CIViC等多个数据库，对体细胞突变进行统一的治疗和耐药水平注释，并采用三级转录本优先策略。该工具为癌症基因组学研究和临床解读提供了可重复的标准化方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: SMART是一个基于Docker的流程，整合OncoKB（含耐药等级R1-R3）、VEP、CIViC等多个数据库，对体细胞突变进行统一的治疗和耐药水平注释，并采用三级转录本优先策略。该工具为癌症基因组学研究和临床解读提供了可重复的标准化方案。

quick_skim.title: LeafRank: A phylodynamic framework for inferring relative fitness from single-cell phylogenies in chromosomally unstable tumors
quick_skim.abstract: Tumors contain cancer cells with diverse growth potentials that shape evolutionary trajectories, yet this fitness diversity remains difficult to quantify in cases of whole-genome duplication (WGD) and chromosomal instability. We present LeafRank, a mathematical framework that leverages single-cell DNA-seq phylogenies to infer the relative fitness of individual cells. Using a multi-type branching process model, LeafRank integrates full tree topology, including branch lengths and bifurcation patterns, to estimate marginal fitness probabilities under punctuated evolutionary regimes driven by rare driver events. To account for elevated aberration rates following WGD, we introduce a tree-rescaling strategy that adjusts for lineage-specific genomic instability. Unlike methods focused on predefined subclones, LeafRank ranks all sampled cells, enabling flexible assessment of growth heterogeneity. Simulations demonstrate high accuracy across spatial and non-spatial virtual tumors. Applied to ovarian cancer, LeafRank reveals directional and parallel selection in WGD tumors and identifies recurrent copy number events enriched in high-fitness lineages. WGD lineages do not show immediate growth advantages but acquire fitness through subsequent alterations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: single-cell phylogeny framework for inferring fitness in tumors, applicable to resistance mechanism studies
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 单细胞系统发育框架推断肿瘤适合度，可应用于耐药机制研究
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 单细胞系统发育框架推断肿瘤适合度，可应用于耐药机制研究
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 单细胞系统发育框架推断肿瘤适合度，可应用于耐药机制研究
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LeafRank uses single-cell DNA-seq phylogenies and a multi-type branching process to infer relative fitness of cancer cells, enabling quantification of clonal advantage in chromosomally unstable tumors.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对染色体不稳定肿瘤中细胞适合度难以量化的问题，本文提出LeafRank，利用单细胞DNA-seq系统发育树和多类型分支过程模型推断单个细胞的相对适合度。该方法整合树拓扑和枝长，估计在稀有驱动事件下的边际适合度概率，并引入树缩放策略校正WGD后的异常率，为研究耐药克隆进化提供了定量工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对染色体不稳定肿瘤中细胞适合度难以量化的问题，本文提出LeafRank，利用单细胞DNA-seq系统发育树和多类型分支过程模型推断单个细胞的相对适合度。该方法整合树拓扑和枝长，估计在稀有驱动事件下的边际适合度概率，并引入树缩放策略校正WGD后的异常率，为研究耐药克隆进化提供了定量工具。

quick_skim.title: User-friendly transcriptomic data analysis with ArrayAnalysis
quick_skim.abstract: Transcriptomic profiling has become a cornerstone of modern biomedical research. To make transcriptomic analyses accessible to a broader scientific community, specifically including researchers with limited bioinformatics expertise, we introduced ArrayAnalysis in 2013 as a user-friendly web-based application for microarray data analysis. We now present a major update (https://arrayanalysis.org), introducing a strongly interactive platform that facilitates the dedicated exploration and analysis of both microarray and RNA-seq data, and allows for the generation of publication-ready outputs. Users can perform key analysis steps, including data pre-processing and quality control, differential expression analysis, and gene set analysis, via a sequential, interactive workflow. At each step, the application provides interactive visualizations accompanied by information pages to support interpretation. Users can dynamically adjust figure layouts and colour palettes and export figures as vector graphics and high-resolution raster images. For non-expert users, ArrayAnalysis offers step-by-step guidance to support correct usage and facilitate learning, while for experienced bioinformaticians, it provides a streamlined and flexible workflow ideal for large-scale analyses requiring efficient and consistent processing. ArrayAnalysis is available both as a web application and for local deployment as a desktop application, Docker image, or R package, making it suitable for diverse computational environments, user groups, and analytical purposes. Together, ArrayAnalysis empowers a broad community of biomedical researchers to unlock the full potential of transcriptomic data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: user-friendly transcriptomic analysis tool enabling resistance research
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用户友好的转录组分析工具，支持耐药研究
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用户友好的转录组分析工具，支持耐药研究
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用户友好的转录组分析工具，支持耐药研究
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ArrayAnalysis update provides interactive web platform for microarray and RNA-seq data analysis, enabling bioinformatics steps including preprocessing, differential expression, and gene set analysis for transcriptomic tumor studies.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: ArrayAnalysis平台重大更新，现可交互分析微阵列和RNA-seq数据，涵盖预处理、差异表达和基因集分析。该工具面向缺乏生物信息学经验的研究者，生成可直接发表的结果。对于肿瘤耐药转录组研究，提供了便捷的分析入口，有助于加速生物标志物发现。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: ArrayAnalysis平台重大更新，现可交互分析微阵列和RNA-seq数据，涵盖预处理、差异表达和基因集分析。该工具面向缺乏生物信息学经验的研究者，生成可直接发表的结果。对于肿瘤耐药转录组研究，提供了便捷的分析入口，有助于加速生物标志物发现。
